key_provisions: AI syllabus statements required in all courses beginning Summer 2025
Default policy: if instructor does not explicitly allow GenAI, it is prohibited
Three permissible usage categories: fully integrated, limited use, prohibited
Third-party AI tools restricted to DCL1 (public) data only
FERPA, HR, health, and human-subjects data prohibited in external tools
ai_policy.summary: Following a Spring 2024 provost-commissioned AI & Learning Environment Task Force (report delivered June 2024), Mizzou adopted a decentralized policy that leaves GenAI use decisions to individual instructors, backed by a mandatory syllabus-statement requirement starting Summer 2025. System-level data guidance restricts third-party tools to DCL1 public data, with sensitive classes of data channeled into approved enterprise tools or the internally-hosted Show-Me AI walled garden.

governance_structure.notes: Established Spring 2025 by MU Faculty Council, the Provost, and the President to advise on AI policy, use, and study across teaching, research, and business practices. Chaired by Kevin Brown (associate professor). Predecessor AI & Learning Environment Task Force (Spring 2024) was tri-chaired by Enid Schatz (Grad School), Tori Mondelli (Teaching for Learning Center), and Ben Trachtenberg (Academic Integrity). A separate 2025-2026 cohort of 14 AI Teaching Fellows serves each college/school.

vendor_deployments.notes: Mizzou-hosted walled-garden proof of concept launched September 2025 for faculty; students added January 2026. ~1,000 pilot users rolled out in waves. Provides model choice across three Claude models, two Gemini models, three ChatGPT models, and Llama 4 Maverick 17B. DCL-3 compliant; data not used for model training. Targeted for full campus availability by 2026-27 academic year.

UM System purchased an Education/Enterprise workspace with data protections and SSO via @umsystem.edu accounts; all university ChatGPT accounts are migrated into the managed workspace so prompts are not used for training.

DoIT evaluation pilot through September 2025 for faculty/staff at $360/user via departmental funds. Restricted in MU Health Care, School of Medicine, and nursing/health-sciences units for security compliance.
Listed as approved enterprise tools with SSO access.

ai_academic_programs.name: Graduate Certificate in AI and Machine Learning
Undergraduate AI Certificate (AI Essentials, Writing with AI, AI Design Studio + elective)

notable_deals_investments.title: UM System Board of Curators approves M.S. and Ph.D. AI programs at MU
notable_deals_investments.counterparty: UM System Board of Curators / MO Department of Higher Education and Workforce Development
notable_deals_investments.summary: Curators approved AI graduate degrees at MU and UMKC in early February 2026; faculty lead Yi Shang is targeting a Fall 2026 launch pending state DHEWD sign-off.

notable_deals_investments.title: Mizzou AI-driven energy efficiency project
notable_deals_investments.summary: Mizzou researchers leading AI-driven building energy efficiency work with potential deployment on campuses nationwide.

leadership_statements.speaker: Matthew Martens
leadership_statements.role: Provost and Executive Vice Chancellor for Academic Affairs
leadership_statements.context: AI Update Fall 2024 and task force report release
leadership_statements.quote_or_summary: Charged the AI and Learning Environment Task Force (Spring 2024) and publicly committed Mizzou to becoming an 'AI forward' institution; encouraged all faculty to add AI syllabus statements for Fall 2024 and funded AI Teaching Fellows in every college for 2025-2026.

leadership_statements.speaker: Kevin Brown
leadership_statements.role: Associate Professor and Chair, MU AI Standing Committee
leadership_statements.context: KBIA coverage of Show-Me AI pilot launch
leadership_statements.quote_or_summary: "We need to have that AI literacy. It really is a new kind of literacy." Framed Show-Me AI as a way to give students individual freedom within a walled-garden environment that preserves academic integrity.

leadership_statements.speaker: Justin Palozola
leadership_statements.context: KBIA coverage of Show-Me AI pilot launch
leadership_statements.quote_or_summary: "It creates that creative environment where faculty and students can create lots of different possibilities." Expects Show-Me AI available to all faculty and staff by 2026-27.

state_ai_policy_context.summary: Governor Mike Kehoe signed EO 26-02 on January 14, 2026, establishing a statewide AI strategic framework; the order directs DHEWD Commissioner Bennett Boggs to evaluate existing AI education programs and recommend new AI-focused degrees, certificates, and apprenticeships, with reports due by Nov 30, 2026. Companion EO 26-03 formalizes the 'Missouri GREAT' initiative encouraging state-agency AI adoption. Broader AI regulation legislation has stalled in 2026 over concerns about losing federal rural broadband funds. At the system level, UM System publishes data-classification-based generative AI guidance through at.umsystem.edu covering all four campuses.

litigation_or_controversy.summary: Student pushback against Show-Me AI pilot: Columbia Missourian reported students criticizing the program as 'anti-education,' raising concerns about academic engagement, racial bias in AI systems, environmental impact, and cost relative to tuition. No formal lawsuit; reputational/opinion controversy only.

narrative_summary: Mizzou is one of the more operationally advanced SEC peers on generative AI, pairing a formal governance stack (Provost-commissioned 2024 task force, Spring 2025 AI Standing Committee, 14 AI Teaching Fellows) with a rare in-house capability: Show-Me AI, a walled-garden, DCL-3-compliant multi-model platform (Claude, Gemini, ChatGPT, Llama 4) launched to ~1,000 pilot users beginning September 2025, with faculty/student rollout targeted for 2026-27. Alongside Show-Me AI, the UM System runs a ChatGPT Edu workspace with SSO and a Microsoft 365 Copilot pilot, and has formally restricted OpenAI Atlas, Otter.AI, and Read AI. Academically, the Board of Curators approved M.S. and Ph.D. AI degrees in February 2026 (targeting Fall 2026), layered on an existing graduate AI/ML certificate and a Spring 2026 undergraduate AI certificate. Policy-wise, Provost Martens has pushed an 'AI forward' stance with mandatory syllabus statements since Summer 2025, while leaving use-permission decisions to individual instructors. State context reinforces this: Governor Kehoe's EO 26-02 (Jan 2026) tasks DHEWD with recommending new AI degrees and apprenticeships. The main risks for a CIO peer-comparison view are (a) visible student opposition to Show-Me AI and (b) no dedicated Chief AI Officer role—AI leadership is distributed across the Provost, CIO Ben Canlas, and the Standing Committee chair.
confidence_notes: HIGH confidence on governance, policy, Show-Me AI, vendor stack, AI degrees, state EO, and leadership identities (all from primary .missouri.edu / umsystem.edu / governor.mo.gov / major local press sources). MEDIUM confidence on exact pilot user counts (Show-Me AI reported as 'about 1,000'; Copilot pilot count not disclosed) and on Copilot pilot announcement date. No evidence found of a dedicated Chief AI Officer, a named NVIDIA partnership, or specific dollar-figure AI investments, so those fields are null. Enrollment is preliminary Fall 2025 figure (~31,300).
